message:
chore(beads): close br-r37-c1-zvfcu (eigenvector_centrality empty)

Fixed in commit 140d71b. Both eigenvector_centrality and
eigenvector_centrality_numpy now raise NetworkXPointlessConcept
with nx's exact wording on the empty graph, instead of silently
returning {}.

Sweep: 6648 passed, 0 failed (+5 new regression tests).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -155,7 +155,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-z2hm3","title":"parity: geometric_soft_configuration_graph silently ran wrong algorithm (random_geometric_graph instead of S^1/H^2 model)","description":"fnx had geometric_soft_configuration_graph(beta=1, n=100, dim=2, pos=None, seed=None) which silently re-routed to random_geometric_graph(n, 0.3) — a completely different model. nx's S^1/H^2 hyperbolic soft configuration model uses signature (*, beta, n=None, gamma=None, mean_degree=None, kappas=None, seed=None) where beta is required. Drop-in callers expecting the documented Krioukov S^1 model got back a uniform random geometric graph (no degree heterogeneity, no hyperbolic structure, wrong density/clustering). Aligned the signature to nx exactly and delegated the implementation to nx so the well-tested upstream algorithm runs end-to-end. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:21:19.401830126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:06.791575501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:06.789049518Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 83b29d5. Signature matches nx (keyword-only, beta required) and implementation delegates to nx so the proper S^1/H^2 hyperbolic model runs end-to-end. Returns fnx.Graph for public type contract. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-z4i7f","title":"parity: eulerian_path raises NotImplemented on directed graphs; nx accepts directed","description":"fnx.eulerian_path(DiGraph) raises NetworkXNotImplemented('not implemented for directed type'). nx supports directed Eulerian paths and raises NetworkXError('Graph has no Eulerian paths.') only when the graph genuinely lacks one. Drop-in code that calls eulerian_path(digraph) breaks. Same pattern as br-r37-c1-bf1wb (has_eulerian_path). Fix: delegate to nx for directed inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:45:43.895735626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:47:52.257570848Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:47:52.255437959Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 05cc661. eulerian_path now accepts directed graphs (delegating to nx for the directed case), matching nx's full contract. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-zud42","title":"parity: community louvain_partitions missing (exposes per-level Louvain hierarchy)","description":"networkx.algorithms.community.louvain_partitions(G, weight=weight, resolution=1, threshold=1e-7, seed=None) yields partitions at each Louvain level. franken_networkx exposes louvain_communities (final partition only) but is missing louvain_partitions which gives intermediate levels for inspection/debugging. Add Python wrapper delegating via _call_networkx_community_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T13:47:44.957693619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T14:22:43.059355524Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T14:22:43.057527854Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9b2ff10: louvain_partitions wrapper; verified karate seed=42 -> 2 levels, partition contents identical to nx","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"br-r37-c1-zvfcu","title":"eigenvector_centrality{,_numpy} silently returns {} on empty graph; nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept","description":"fnx.eigenvector_centrality(empty_graph) and fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(empty_graph) silently return an empty dict {}. nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept('cannot compute centrality for the null graph'). Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept) won't trigger on fnx.

Repro:
  >>> fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
  {}
  >>> nx.eigenvector_centrality(nx.Graph())
  NetworkXPointlessConcept: cannot compute centrality for the null graph

Survey of other centralities on empty graphs (degree, in_degree, out_degree, closeness, betweenness, harmonic, load, subgraph, communicability_betweenness, information, current_flow_*, pagerank, hits, katz, katz_numpy) all match nx's behavior — only eigenvector_centrality and eigenvector_centrality_numpy are off.

Fix: add explicit empty-graph guard at the top of both wrappers that raises NetworkXPointlessConcept with nx's wording, before falling through to the Rust/numpy implementation that swallows the case.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:01.797714267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:33.169331648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-zvfcu","title":"eigenvector_centrality{,_numpy} silently returns {} on empty graph; nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept","description":"fnx.eigenvector_centrality(empty_graph) and fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(empty_graph) silently return an empty dict {}. nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept('cannot compute centrality for the null graph'). Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept) won't trigger on fnx.

Repro:
  >>> fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
  {}
  >>> nx.eigenvector_centrality(nx.Graph())
  NetworkXPointlessConcept: cannot compute centrality for the null graph

Survey of other centralities on empty graphs (degree, in_degree, out_degree, closeness, betweenness, harmonic, load, subgraph, communicability_betweenness, information, current_flow_*, pagerank, hits, katz, katz_numpy) all match nx's behavior — only eigenvector_centrality and eigenvector_centrality_numpy are off.

Fix: add explicit empty-graph guard at the top of both wrappers that raises NetworkXPointlessConcept with nx's wording, before falling through to the Rust/numpy implementation that swallows the case.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:01.797714267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:51:57.030438515Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T05:51:57.027716017Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-01","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] signature mismatches blocked nx drop-in kwarg calls (8 functions)","description":"A signature sweep comparing every fnx public function against its networkx counterpart surfaced real drop-in breakage. Missing nx-style kwargs caused TypeErrors on documented calls:

- fiedler_vector: missing tol, method, seed
- spectral_ordering: missing weight, tol, method, seed
- spectral_bisection: missing normalized, tol, method, seed
- incidence_matrix: missing keyword-only dtype
- attr_matrix: missing order (numpy memory layout) AND wrong return shape — fnx always returned a (matrix, labels) tuple, but nx returns just the matrix when rc_order is supplied
- attr_sparse_matrix: same return-shape defect as attr_matrix
- has_path: first positional named 'graph'; nx names it 'G' (broke has_path(G=x,...))
- harmonic_diameter / hyper_wiener_index: missing weight
- duplication_divergence_graph: missing create_using
- bfs_labeled_edges: parameter named 'source' (single) instead of nx's 'sources' (iterable)
- dfs_labeled_edges: missing keyword-only sort_neighbors
- is_connected_dominating_set: second param named 'S' instead of nx's 'nbunch'

All fixed to accept the same kwargs as networkx; where semantically meaningful (weight on wiener/harmonic, dtype on incidence_matrix, order on attr_matrix) the kwarg now affects the result. Others (tol/method/seed on the dense spectral solvers) are accepted for signature parity and noted as ignored by the deterministic numpy.linalg.eigh path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed signatures + return shapes. Verified each fixed function with nx-style kwargs; existing parity tests still pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-02","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] to_prufer_sequence panicked (Rust) on non-tree / bad labels instead of raising NotATree","description":"fnx.to_prufer_sequence(G) forwarded straight to the Rust helper which panicked with 'tree invariant: leaf must exist' when G wasn't a tree. networkx raises NotATree (a NetworkXException subclass) in that case, plus NetworkXPointlessConcept for <2 nodes and KeyError when node labels aren't {0..n-1}. PanicException bypasses ordinary except blocks in user code. Fixed by validating is_directed / node count / is_tree / node labels in Python before calling into Rust, matching nx's exception contract exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: validate in Python, raise NetworkXPointlessConcept / NotATree / KeyError as appropriate; verified on karate_club_graph, empty graph, and string-labelled trees — all raise the same exception types as nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-03","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] bethe_hessian_matrix: wrong return type AND wrong values","description":"Two defects in fnx.bethe_hessian_matrix: (1) returned a legacy scipy.sparse.csr_matrix while nx returns csr_array — isinstance(r, csr_array) was False. (2) Computed A = adjacency(G, weight=None) and regularizer r from A.sum(axis=1), both unweighted, but nx uses the *weighted* adjacency for A and D and the *unweighted* degrees for r. On karate_club_graph (integer edge weights) fnx diverged from nx by up to ~465 in matrix entries. Fixed by building A via to_scipy_sparse_array(G, nodelist, format='csr') (weighted by default), computing r from G.degree(v) per-node (unweighted), and using sparse eye_array/diags_array throughout so the final product is a csr_array. Verified on karate, cycle5, K4, havel_hakimi; spectrum matches too.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed type (csr_array) and values. Verified across weighted and unweighted fixtures; spectrum matches; existing tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
